ENGLISH: The Molecular Bioscanner tool is now online and fully operational. We can start gathering information about the local fauna and flora species.
A new biome is now available for survey with the Orbital Scanner.
Armory construction started.
Communications Hub construction started.
Alien Research Laboratory construction finished.
Orbital Scanner construction is in progress.
Orbital Scanner construction finished. We can now use it to Rift Jump to new locations by selecting new mission coordinates through the Orbital Scanner interface.
Initiating construction of the Prime Rift Station.
Energy Pylon construction started.
Ashley, I have made an interesting discovery. Orbital Scanner data suggests that we could find rich Palladium deposits in Galatea's northern Cryo Fields.
Oh, that might be a more friendly biome to visit than the Acidic Plains. Did you find any particular location that we should scout there?
No, but you can search for promising mission areas within the northern Cryo Fields biome directly through the Orbital Scanner Interface.

Rift jump successful. Extreme weather conditions detected. Outside temperatures in this area can dip below the 170 Kelvin threshold. Be careful, Ashley. Such low temperatures may result in decreased structural integrity of our buildings.
Understood. The local fauna may prove to be more dangerous than we could otherwise expect it to be. Lets see what else this biome has to offer.
Atmospheric cooling detected. Snowfall is likely.
Incoming snow clouds detected. Wind speeds are rising slightly.
The air’s cooling off. Feels like snow’s coming.
Clouds are rolling in, and the wind’s picking up a bit.
Light snowfall confirmed. Environmental conditions remain within safe limits.
Snowfall ongoing. Moderate winds detected.
Light snow and some wind. Could be worse.
Snow’s falling now. Wind’s kicking up, but it’s manageable.
Precipitation has stopped. Environmental conditions stabilizing.
Snowfall concluded. Visibility and wind levels returning to normal.
Snow’s stopped. Looks calm again.
Looks like the weather’s clearing up.
Warning. Super-dense cloud systems detected. High-mass ice fragments likely to fall.
Warning. Atmospheric matter is condensing into unstable ice structures above the area.
The clouds are turning solid. Looks like we’re about to get ice falling from the sky.
That doesn’t look like normal weather. Big pieces of ice are forming up above us.
Danger. Atmospheric matter has solidified into falling ice masses.
Danger. Anomalous clouds have condensed into massive ice formations. Icefall in progress.
That’s not snow! Huge chunks of ice are falling out of the clouds!
Those clouds are dropping massive slabs of ice! Take cover!
Anomalous cloud structures dispersing. Icefall activity has ceased.
Atmospheric density returning to normal. No further ice impacts detected.
That was brutal. I can’t believe how much ice came down.
The sky’s clearing. That was way worse than a blizzard.
Conditions indicate an incoming blizzard. Expect extreme cold and reduced visibility.
Warning. Temperature and pressure readings indicate an incoming blizzard.
Temperature’s dropping fast. This might turn into a blizzard.
I don’t like the look of those clouds. Feels like a blizzard’s on the way.
Sustained high winds and extreme cold confirmed. Blizzard ongoing.
Blizzard event underway. Atmospheric instability increasing.
The storm’s here. I can barely see anything out there.
Alright, this just turned into a serious snowstorm.
Extreme weather event concluded. Temperature and visibility are improving.
Cold front dissipated. Weather conditions returning to normal.
I can see again. That blizzard’s over.
The snow’s letting up. Let’s get back to work.
Warning. Atmospheric instability detected. Destructive snow devils likely.
Warning. Snow and wind conditions indicate imminent vortex formation.
I’m seeing snow whip into tight spirals. This could get dangerous.
The wind’s getting violent. That snow’s starting to spin hard.
Danger. Snow devil event in progress. Structural damage likely.
Danger. Severe rotational winds detected. Snow devils impacting the surface.
A large snow devil has just finished forming! We have to stay away from its path.
A snow devil is moving our way! These things hit way harder than they look!
Extreme surface winds subsiding. Environmental stability returning.
Snow devil event concluded. No further vortex activity detected.
The wind’s finally calming down. That was rough.
Looks like it’s over. I don’t want to see that again anytime soon.
Warning. A Solar Burn event is approaching.
Warning. Solar radiation levels are rising.
The sun’s flaring up. Radiation levels are climbing.
I am getting some serious solar output readings. A Solar Burn is coming.
A Solar Burn event is in progress. Photovoltaic generators are operating at peak efficiency.
Sun activity is at its peak right now. Solar energy generation at maximum levels.
Ashley, my sensors have picked up an unusual gravitational anomaly nearby. I think it might be worth investigating.
Roger that, Mr. Riggs. Let's go check it out.
Wow, look at this ironium deposit. It's absolutely gigantic.
I agree, Ashley. It is much bigger than any we have encountered so far. Unfortunately, we will not be able to extract this ore with our standard Ironium Factories.

That's ok, Mr. Riggs. We can build Drone Mines nearby. They are not as fast as standard automated factories, but they have a long operating range, and they can extract all types of mineable resources. They are slower, but much more versatile.
That makes sense. We should try to maximize the effectiveness of Drone Mines by covering as many additional ore deposits with their range as possible.
Hey, look at that rock over there. It has a very peculiar shape, and I think I see a faint glow coming from one if it's sides. Let's go check it out.
Ok, now tell me this doesn't look like a skull to you.
Affirmative. This does not look like a skull to me.
Come on. Are you serious? It's got obvious eye sockets, teeth, and a nose.

I understand what your brain is telling you, but this most certainly is not a skull. This rock formation is typical of this area. Its geological composition also does not reveal anything out of the ordinary.
What about the glow that's coming from inside it?

There is a small cavern under this rock that's filled with the Florium Cibarium Corulus plant. This species has particularly strong bioluminescence. It uses it as a lure to attract insects and other small creatures for pollination and seed dispersal. You have just proven its effectiveness.
This is as much interesting as it is disappointing. Well, I guess there's nothing more to look at here. Carry on.
